items.title: Profit-taking, MidEast hostilities drag crypto lower after bullish week
items.description: Crypto's weekend gains gave way to a Monday selloff as Middle East tensions resurfaced, South Korea's Kospi lost 9.2% and $253 million in leveraged positions were wiped out.

items.title: Paradigm shifts vs bubbles: AI chips and bitcoin show powerful trends can still produce severe corrections
items.description: Structural change can create lasting opportunities, but explosive rallies in semiconductors, metals and bitcoin show how quickly strong narratives can become speculative excess.

items.title: U.S. inflation, second-quarter earnings reports: Crypto Week Ahead
items.description: Your look at what's coming in the week starting July 13.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin slips below $63,000 in an Asian-session leverage flush
items.description: The liquidations were minor, running at about a sixth of what the market saw at its worst over the past 30 days, per CoinGlass.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $63,800 as war-driven selloff hits everything but crypto
items.description: Gold, oil, stocks and bonds all moved sharply on the fourth round of U.S. strikes on Iran, but bitcoin is little-changed.

items.title: Signs of life?: State of Crypto
items.description: Several sources told CoinDesk that a new draft of the Clarity Act may drop this week, but challenges remain.

items.title: Stablecoin market cap has shrunk by $10 billion since May, but analyst sees no reason to panic
items.description: The market shrank by $7.7 billion in June alone, the largest dollar amount since May 2022's Terra-Luna crash, but stablecoins will likely resume their long-term growth, one analyst said.

items.title: Binance June futures volume at $1.6T defies crypto spot trading slump
items.description: Binance futures volume jumped 80% in June, to $1.61 trillion, outpacing rivals while broader crypto trading activity remained weak.

items.title: BTC price bull market to begin in September? Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
items.description: Bitcoin price analysis called for the end of the bear market within three months as the US-Iran war sent BTC lower from strengthening $64,000 resistance.

items.title: Japan’s SBI to launch yen stablecoin lending with 3% yield
items.description: SBI VC Trade will open JPYSC lending applications on July 16, offering an initial 3% annual rate for a 12-week term without deposit insurance.

items.title: Japan stablecoin payments advance with Lawson trial, Netstars launch
items.description: Lawson will test yen stablecoin payments in Tokyo as Netstars launches a merchant service supporting USDC, USDT and JPYC.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: AI microbusinesses could drive $262B in stablecoin volume by 2033: Swyftx
items.description: The AI-native cohort of the expanding gig economy could increasingly use stablecoins to avoid slow and expensive traditional payment rails, Australian crypto exchange Swyftx said.

items.title: Bank of Thailand targets USDT and cash flows in gray money crackdown
items.description: Thailand has been plagued by Chinese-affiliated scam centers, with illicit gains flowing through a “gray money” economy.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs draw $197M, snap 8-week outflow streak
items.description: Analysts are not yet ready to call it a recovery in institutional demand for Bitcoin.

items.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes as Strait of Hormuz standoff escalates
items.description: Iran responded to a fresh wave of strikes by U.S. forces by launching an attack on American military bases in several Gulf states.

items.title: Meta's Louisiana data center investment to reach $50 billion, aided by generous tax incentives
items.description: Meta said the planned Hyperion data center supercluster in Richland Parish, Louisiana, will be a 5GW facility and cost over $50 billion.

items.title: Graham’s death complicates myriad GOP goals in Congress
items.description: South Carolina Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham died unexpectedly at 71.

items.title: Kalshi launches 'Pro' product for users trading multiple markets at same time, perpetual futures
items.description: The platform is designed to alleviate problems the company's most active traders have run into, according to a memo provided to CNBC.

items.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman spar on X after Apple files OpenAI lawsuit
items.description: Sam Altman insisted that Elon Musk was again obsessed with him because of an OpenAI model release earlier this week.

items.title: TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, reports 68% surge in June revenue
items.description: Ahead of its second-quarter earnings, TSMC reported its June and first-half revenue for 2026.

items.title: Burnout, frustration and heartbreak: Amazon layoffs take their toll in saturated job market
items.description: In the eight-plus months since Amazon announced its most expansive job cuts ever, laid off workers have been thrust into an increasingly saturated labor market.

items.title: A tiny GLP-1 implant is the latest bet to help patients maintain their weight loss
items.description: Vivani Medical is developing an implant of semaglutide, the active ingredient in Novo Nordisk's obesity injection Wegovy and diabetes counterpart Ozempic.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8IExFvX3LVo! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: ASML is kicking off tech earnings. Here’s what to expect from Europe’s biggest semiconductor supplier.
items.description: The Dutch semiconductor-equipment manufacturer is reporting its second-quarter results on Wednesday, with analysts expecting a 15% rise in earnings per share year-over-year.

items.title: Why Citigroup is the one to watch when banks report earnings this week
items.description: Among the largest U.S. banks, Citigroup is expected to show the greatest improvement by one important measure. But it still has a long way to go to reach its own performance target.

items.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
items.description: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

items.title: Oil prices surge as much as 5% after Iran declares Strait of Hormuz is closed
items.description: The week is kicking off with fresh spikes for oil prices as the U.S. and Iran continued to exchange attacks.

items.title: My wife’s 2011 Ford Fiesta was totaled. Should we accept a $2,000 insurance settlement or give up the car for $2,700?
items.description: “The hood is bent, the radiator is cracked, and the front bumper is destroyed.”

items.title: Geopolitical shock exposes SK Hynix leverage after hot ADR debut
items.description: Just one trading session after a highly successful U.S. listing for its depositary receipts, local shares in South Korean chip maker SK Hynix plummeted 15% on the domestic exchange in Seoul.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Global tech stocks fall as Asian memory chipmakers hammered
items.description: Oil jumps as US-Iran hostilities escalate and investors go into ‘risk-off’ mode

items.title: China-proofing European cleantech is a tricky business
items.description: Crude tariffs won’t be enough to promote EU renewable energy

items.title: Trump’s plan for Europe is falling apart
items.description: The US president has a genius for alienating existing and potential allies

items.title: US launches more strikes on Iran as ceasefire teeters
items.description: Fighting escalates as Trump insists Strait of Hormuz remains open while Tehran says it is closed

items.title: Ukraine’s drone swarms test Moscow air defences
items.description: Kyiv intensifies deep-strike campaign against Russia after attacks on refineries sparked fuel crisis

items.title: Legal sector regulator in England and Wales has ‘lost its way’, review finds
items.description: Report into Legal Services Board follows a number of high-profile law firm collapses in recent years

items.title: EU moves towards social media ban for children under 13
items.description: Brussels will propose gradual access for different ages in response to concerns over child safety online

items.title: Olly Robbins takes legal action over sacking in Mandelson scandal
items.description: Former Foreign Office chief says Keir Starmer’s actions were ‘unlawful’ and ‘unreasonable’

items.title: Economists Warn of A.I. Threat
items.description: Nearly 200 economists signed a letter calling for policymakers to do more to understand and respond to potential A.I. disruptions.

items.title: Europe Takes Step Toward Possible Social Media Ban for Children
items.description: After the release of a new report, the European Commission is considering changing the rules across the 27-nation bloc.

items.title: How Wealthy People Are Getting to World Cup Games
items.description: The skies above the Hamptons are packed with choppers headed to games, as “inequality is taking it right in the face.”

items.title: 5 New Roller Coasters to Make You Scream This Summer
items.description: Let the adrenaline flow on coasters that will shoot you high in the air, spin you around and turn you upside down. You’ll definitely want a second ride.

items.title: U.A.W. President Says He’s the Victim of ‘Bogus Allegations’
items.description: A grand jury is investigating whether Shawn Fain used his position to seek favors for his fiancée and retaliated against the union official who denied them.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge After Iran and U.S. Trade Strikes
items.description: The renewed hostilities posed fresh risks to ships seeking to navigate the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: States Prepare Lawsuit to Block Paramount’s Merger With Warner Bros.
items.description: State attorneys general are said to be getting ready to file a lawsuit as soon as this week to halt the $111 billion deal, a potential major obstacle.

items.title: ‘Moana’ Falters at the Box Office, Casting Doubt on Disney’s Formula
items.description: The live-action remake took in roughly $43 million over the weekend in North America, about 30 percent less than Disney had expected.
